title: Gil Cassel
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Cassel}} {{Character |type=Sith Empire |image=[[File:Gil_Cassel2.jpg]] |name=Gil Cassel |homeworld=[[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]][[Legacy (2006) 4|''Legacy'' (2006) 4]] |birth= |death=[[137 ABY]], [[Borosk/Legends|Borosk]] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Gray]] |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Fel Empire]] *[[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] **[[407th Stormtrooper Division]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Lord Malevel… fighting against other stormtroopers doesn't sit well with our ''own'' troops. A surrender would prevent needless loss of lives and materiel.|Gil Cassel, to Darth Maleval|}} '''Gil Cassel''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] born on [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]] to a pro-[[Fel Empire|Imperial]] family. He was the brother of [[Jared Cassel]], who, like Gil, joined the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|There are some things more important than duty.… than life…''"
"''Then let ''both'' of you die as ''traitors''!|Gil Cassel and Darth Maleval|}} Gil Cassel was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]] whose family was pro-[[Fel Empire|Imperial]]. He and his brother, [[Jared Cassel]], both joined the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] under [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]. After [[Darth Krayt]], [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], seized the Imperial throne, Gil Cassel remained loyal to the [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Imperial faction]] while his brother defected to Fel's [[Empire-in-exile]]. [[File:Gil_Cassel.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Gil Cassel.]] By [[137 ABY]], Cassel had reached the rank [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] and served in the [[407th Stormtrooper Division]], which was stationed in [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]]. Meanwhile, his brother served in the [[908th Stormtrooper Division]] and held the rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]]. As part of the [[Second Imperial Civil War]] and as a result of the 908th's defection, the 407th was sent to [[Borosk/Legends|Borosk]] to eliminate the 908th. Despite the presence of his brother in the enemy's side, Gil nonetheless led his troops in [[Battle of Borosk (137 ABY)|battle]]. In the midst of the battle, with the 407th already winning ground to the 908th, Cassel and [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Ran Harkas]] of [[Joker Squad]], discussed the possibility of offering the defecting 908th a chance to surrender. However, [[Darth Maleval]], the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] overseeing the 407th on behalf of Emperor Krayt, refused the plan and ordered the complete eradication of the 908th. Despite objections from [[Jes Gistang]] and [[Hondo Karr]], both members of Joker Squad, Lieutenant Cassel chose to follow Lord Maleval's orders. After the 407th's victory, [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Maleval brought the captured Jared Cassel to be executed by Gil as a test of his loyalty to the Sith Empire. However. Gil refused to kill his own brother, for which both were decapitated by the [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] Sith Lord. ==Personality and traits== Gil Cassel had [[Color/Legends|gray]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], blue [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and a light skin tone. ==Appearances== *[[Legacy (2006) 4|''Legacy'' (2006) 4]] {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Gil Cassel |pl=Gil Cassel }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Cassel, Gil}} [[Category:Bastion residents]] [[Category:Fel Empire military personnel]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Army officers of Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
